messages.content:
Question: In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

Available databases: core_crm, sales_pipeline, activities, products_Orders, territory, support_tickets, email_database, knowledge_base

Available schema:
Database: core_crm (sqlite)
  Table: User — Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
  Table: Account — Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
  Table: Contact — Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId
Database: sales_pipeline (duckdb)
  Table: Contract — Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
  Table: Lead — Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
  Table: Opportunity — Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
  Table: OpportunityLineItem — Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
  Table: Quote — Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
  Table: QuoteLineItem — Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice
Database: activities (duckdb)
  Table: Event — Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
  Table: Task — Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
  Table: VoiceCallTranscript__c — Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c
Database: products_Orders (sqlite)
  Table: ProductCategory — Id, Name, CatalogId
  Table: Product2 — Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
  Table: ProductCategoryProduct — Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
  Table: Pricebook2 — Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
  Table: PricebookEntry — Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
  Table: Order — (no columns)
  Table: OrderItem — Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId
Database: territory (sqlite)
  Table: Territory2 — Id, Name, Description
  Table: UserTerritory2Association — Id, UserId, Territory2Id
Database: support_tickets (postgres)
  Table: Case — id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
  Table: casehistory__c — id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
  Table: emailmessage — id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
  Table: issue__c — id, name, description__c
  Table: knowledge__kav — id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
  Table: livechattranscript — id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid
Database: email_database (postgres)
  Table: Case — id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
  Table: casehistory__c — id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
  Table: emailmessage — id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
  Table: issue__c — id, name, description__c
  Table: knowledge__kav — id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
  Table: livechattranscript — id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid
Database: knowledge_base (postgres)
  Table: Case — id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
  Table: casehistory__c — id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
  Table: emailmessage — id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
  Table: issue__c — id, name, description__c
  Table: knowledge__kav — id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
  Table: livechattranscript — id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

function.name: execute_python
function.arguments:
{"code": "import pandas as pd
import json

# We need to find the agent with the lowest average handle time for cases in the past four months (from 2023-09-02, so >= 2023-05-02).
# Handle time = Case Closed time - Case Creation time.
# We only consider cases that have NOT been transferred.
# A case is NOT transferred if it has exactly ONE 'Owner Assignment'.
# The agent who handled it is the newvalue__c of that 'Owner Assignment'.
# We need agents who processed MORE THAN ONE case (>= 2 cases).

query = """
WITH case_assignments AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, COUNT(*) as num_assignments, MAX(newvalue__c) as agent_id
    FROM casehistory__c
    WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    GROUP BY caseid__c
),
untransferred_cases AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, agent_id
    FROM case_assignments
    WHERE num_assignments = 1
),
case_times AS (
    SELECT 
        c.caseid__c,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Creation' THEN c.createddate END) as creation_time,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Closed' THEN c.createddate END) as closed_time
    FROM casehistory__c c
    WHERE c.field__c IN ('Case Creation', 'Case Closed')
    GROUP BY c.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    u.agent_id,
    COUNT(u.caseid__c) as num_cases,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(t.creation_time AS TIMESTAMP)))) as avg_handle_time
FROM untransferred_cases u
JOIN case_times t ON u.caseid__c = t.caseid__c
WHERE t.closed_time IS NOT NULL 
  AND t.creation_time IS NOT NULL
  AND CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) >= '2023-05-02'
  AND CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) <= '2023-09-02'
GROUP BY u.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(u.caseid__c) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC
LIMIT 5;
"""
"}

function.arguments:
{"query": "WITH case_assignments AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, COUNT(*) as num_assignments, MAX(newvalue__c) as agent_id
    FROM casehistory__c
    WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    GROUP BY caseid__c
),
untransferred_cases AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, agent_id
    FROM case_assignments
    WHERE num_assignments = 1
),
case_times AS (
    SELECT 
        c.caseid__c,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Creation' THEN c.createddate END) as creation_time,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Closed' THEN c.createddate END) as closed_time
    FROM casehistory__c c
    WHERE c.field__c IN ('Case Creation', 'Case Closed')
    GROUP BY c.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    u.agent_id,
    COUNT(u.caseid__c) as num_cases,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(t.creation_time AS TIMESTAMP)))) as avg_handle_time
FROM untransferred_cases u
JOIN case_times t ON u.caseid__c = t.caseid__c
WHERE t.closed_time IS NOT NULL 
  AND t.creation_time IS NOT NULL
  AND CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) >= '2023-05-02'
  AND CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) <= '2023-09-02'
GROUP BY u.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(u.caseid__c) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC
LIMIT 5;", "database": "support_tickets", "query_type": "sql"}

ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC
LIMIT 5;"}

function.name: query_db
function.arguments:
{"query_type": "sql", "database": "support_tickets", "query": "WITH case_assignments AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, COUNT(*) as num_assignments, MAX(newvalue__c) as agent_id
    FROM casehistory__c
    WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
    GROUP BY caseid__c
),
untransferred_cases AS (
    SELECT caseid__c, agent_id
    FROM case_assignments
    WHERE num_assignments = 1
),
case_times AS (
    SELECT 
        c.caseid__c,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Creation' THEN c.createddate END) as creation_time,
        MAX(CASE WHEN c.field__c = 'Case Closed' THEN c.createddate END) as closed_time
    FROM casehistory__c c
    WHERE c.field__c IN ('Case Creation', 'Case Closed')
    GROUP BY c.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    u.agent_id,
    COUNT(u.caseid__c) as num_cases,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(t.closed_time AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(t.creation_time AS TIMESTAMP)))) as avg_handle_time
FROM untransferred_cases u
JOIN case_times t ON u.caseid__c = t.caseid__c
WHERE t.closed_time IS NOT NULL 
  AND t.creation_time IS NOT NULL
  AND CAST(t.creation_time AS TIMESTAMP) >= '2023-05-02'
  AND CAST(t.creation_time AS TIMESTAMP) <= '2023-09-02'
GROUP BY u.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(u.caseid__c) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC
LIMIT 5;"}

question:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02
